file_insights.details: set_cooldown(), in_cooldown(default 5min), get_cooldown_remaining() - these methods were referenced in scalp_fast.py but were missing from StateManager implementation, representing a gap from subtask-2-3

Tests StateManager initialization with no existing state, verifies lazy file creation on first save, validates schema (version, active_trades, cooldowns, watch_state, scan_timestamps), confirms all field types are dicts, tests cooldown operations and scan timestamp tracking using local temp directory
Plan now tracks that fresh start test was implemented and all validation passed

file_insights.change: Added detailed progress entry for subtask-5-1
file_insights.details: Documented bug discovery (missing methods), fix implementation, test results, and verification outcomes

patterns_discovered.name: Lazy Initialization
patterns_discovered.description: StateManager initializes without creating state file; file created automatically on first save operation
patterns_discovered.evidence: Test shows file doesn't exist after init but exists after save_cooldown() call

patterns_discovered.name: Convenience Wrapper Methods
patterns_discovered.description: Adding domain-specific wrapper methods with sensible defaults over low-level APIs
patterns_discovered.evidence: set_cooldown(symbol, minutes) wraps save_cooldown(); in_cooldown(symbol, minutes=5) wraps check_cooldown()

patterns_discovered.name: Schema Validation Pattern
patterns_discovered.description: Integration tests verify not just functionality but also correct schema structure and field types
patterns_discovered.evidence: Test checks state file contains required fields with correct types (all dicts) after operations

patterns_discovered.name: Local Testing Pattern
patterns_discovered.description: Using temporary directories for tests instead of home directory to avoid permission issues

gotchas_discovered.description: scalp_fast.py was calling in_cooldown(), set_cooldown(), and get_cooldown_remaining() on StateManager but these methods did not exist
gotchas_discovered.root_cause: Subtask-2-3 verification only checked for import errors, not actual runtime method calls
gotchas_discovered.resolution: Added 3 missing convenience methods to StateManager

gotchas_discovered.description: Previous subtask verification tested import success but didn't catch missing method calls that would fail at runtime
gotchas_discovered.impact: Method gaps only discovered during actual integration testing in subtask-5-1
gotchas_discovered.lesson: Verification must include both import checks and representative method calls

gotchas_discovered.description: Testing with ~/.tv_scanner_state.json can cause permission/ownership problems in some environments
gotchas_discovered.solution: Use local temp directories for tests; production code can use home directory

key_achievements: Identified and fixed missing StateManager convenience methods
Created comprehensive integration test covering all fresh start scenarios
Verified lazy initialization behavior works correctly
Confirmed state file schema is valid after operations
Verified atomic writes persist state changes immediately
All convenience methods (set_cooldown, in_cooldown, get_cooldown_remaining) working correctly
scalp_fast.py now imports successfully with updated StateManager
outcome_details.validation_results: ✓ StateManager initializes cleanly with no existing state; ✓ State file created on first save with valid schema; ✓ All required fields present with correct types; ✓ All state operations work from fresh start; ✓ State changes persist immediately via atomic writes

recommendations.recommendation: Add static/runtime method validation to catch missing method implementations earlier
recommendations.rationale: Current approach (import check) missed 3 missing methods that only surfaced during integration testing

recommendations.recommendation: Create integration test suite that actually exercises all referenced methods, not just imports
recommendations.rationale: Would have caught the missing convenience methods before subtask-5-1

recommendations.recommendation: Document lazy initialization behavior prominently in StateManager docstring
recommendations.rationale: Helps future developers understand when state file is created vs. when it's initialized in memory

recommendations.recommendation: Use temporary directories for all filesystem-based tests to avoid environment-specific permission issues
recommendations.rationale: Makes tests portable and prevents failures in restrictive permission environments
